project_priority_statement: First canonical public record of Proof-of-Observation inside the JANUS research line.
global_priority_boundary: No global historical-priority claim is made without a separate literature and prior-art review.
definition: An observer and evidence-gating method that records machine telemetry in an integrity-linked chain, separates facts from derived metrics and claims, requires explicit provenance and exact exposure for rate claims, uses independent holdouts, and fails closed when evidence is incomplete.
canonical_principles: Do not trust an impression. Trust the chain of observations.
Hash-chain integrity protects history from silent rewriting; claim gates protect understanding from self-deception.
Assigned work is not completed work.
A beautiful heatmap is not proof.
Do not predict the hash. Test whether the tails preserve conditional structure.

organism_state_model.memory: NAS, DB, JSONL and persistence availability
organism_state_model.tail: rare-tail telemetry, exposure, controls and claim gates

implementation_lineage.name: EXACT_EXPOSURE_SHADOW_EMITTER
implementation_lineage.purpose: Emit disabled-by-default exact integer hashes_attempted deltas after proven completed hash batches, bound to frozen coordinate keys and sealed windows.

forbidden_public_claims: a visual cluster proves causal advantage
a valid hash chain makes source telemetry true
current_scientific_boundary: Tail events and their recorded coordinates are observable, but exact historical per-window and per-coordinate exposure is not recoverable from the current artifacts; therefore exposure-normalized rate and advantage gates remain closed.
